text.title: Maintain Mode SOPs Solo Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Maintain Mode SOPs Solo application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: SOP MUST define exactly four cadences: weekly (≤30 min), monthly (≤2h), quarterly (≤4h), incident-driven on-call. Five cadences is rejected as overengineered; three is rejected as under-coverage.
rule.rationale: Four-cadence model captures the natural rhythm of maintain-mode without exploding into a build-mode plan.

rule.statement: Each cadence MUST list named, owned tasks; free-floating 'check stuff' is rejected.
rule.rationale: Un-named tasks rot; named tasks survive operator memory loss.

rule.statement: On-call rule MUST define the trigger (status-page red / customer email / monitor alert) and the max-response window.
rule.rationale: Without trigger + window, on-call becomes 'I'll get to it' — customers churn silently.

rule.statement: All cadences MUST be calendar-locked (recurring invites + ICS exports); ad-hoc cadences are rejected.
rule.rationale: Calendar-locked cadences survive context-switching, ad-hoc ones don't.

rule.statement: SOP MUST name the sunset trigger (MRR < X for N months OR churn > Y%) — i.e. when maintain-mode flips to sunset-mode.
rule.rationale: Without sunset trigger, maintain-mode becomes perpetual neglect.
